generations.label: 4th Gen
generations.common_competitors: Honda Civic, Toyota Corolla, Volkswagen Jetta

known_issues.title: Carbon buildup on intake valves
known_issues.description: Like most direct-injection engines, the SkyActiv-G 2.5L can accumulate carbon on the intake valves over time, leading to a rough idle and reduced throttle response.
known_issues.symptoms: A rough or uneven idle, hesitation when accelerating from a stop, and slightly reduced fuel economy.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask about idle quality and whether a walnut-shell intake cleaning has ever been performed.

known_issues.title: Mazda Connect infotainment freezing
known_issues.description: The infotainment system can freeze or fail to boot, occasionally requiring a full system reset.
known_issues.symptoms: The infotainment display freezing on one screen or failing to boot at startup, sometimes requiring the ignition to be cycled to recover.
known_issues.recommendation: Cycle through navigation, audio, and phone pairing screens using the console dial during the test drive.

known_issues.title: Clutch chatter on manual transmission models
known_issues.description: 6MT-equipped cars can develop clutch chatter or grabbiness from a stop as the clutch disc wears.
known_issues.symptoms: A juddering or chattering sensation through the pedal and chassis when releasing the clutch from a stop.
known_issues.recommendation: Take several stop-and-go launches on the test drive to check for chatter or slipping.

known_issues.title: AWD rear differential clutch pack wear
known_issues.description: On i-Activ AWD models, the rear differential clutch pack can wear prematurely, reducing torque transfer in slippery conditions.
known_issues.symptoms: Reduced traction or a slight slip-and-grab feeling when accelerating on wet, snowy, or gravel surfaces.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask about AWD fluid change history and test the car on a wet or gravel surface if possible.

generations.label: 2nd Gen
generations.common_competitors: Honda CR-V, Toyota RAV4, Nissan Rogue

known_issues.title: Fuel dilution in oil on turbo models
known_issues.description: The 2.5L Turbo engine, offered from 2019 onward, can experience gasoline diluting the engine oil on short, cold-weather trips.
known_issues.symptoms: A gasoline smell on the oil dipstick and an oil level that appears to rise slightly between changes.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask about oil change intervals and check the oil for a gasoline smell on the dipstick.

known_issues.title: Carbon buildup on intake valves
known_issues.description: Direct injection leads to carbon accumulation on the intake valves over time, causing a rough idle and hesitation.
known_issues.symptoms: A rough or uneven idle and a slight hesitation or flat spot when accelerating from low RPM.
known_issues.recommendation: Check idle smoothness at a stop and ask about any intake cleaning service history.

known_issues.title: Infotainment lag and Bluetooth dropouts
known_issues.description: The Mazda Connect system can lag when switching between screens and occasionally drops Bluetooth phone connections.
known_issues.symptoms: A delay when switching infotainment screens and Bluetooth calls or audio cutting out mid-drive.
known_issues.recommendation: Pair a phone and test Bluetooth audio and calls during the test drive.

known_issues.title: Brake pedal soft or spongy feel
known_issues.description: Some owners report a spongy brake pedal that firms back up after a fluid flush and bleed.
known_issues.symptoms: The brake pedal feeling soft or requiring extra travel before the brakes engage firmly.
known_issues.recommendation: Ask when the brake fluid was last flushed and test firm braking on the drive.
